data.description:
## Problem

On first launch, new users are hit with a cascade of macOS permission prompts (Documents / Desktop / Downloads / iCloud Drive / removable volumes) the instant they open c11. The prior TCC primer (merged in #55 / #56) was supposed to contextualize these — but in practice the race is already lost by the time the primer appears.

Flow in `Sources/AppDelegate.swift:6202-6218` (`sendWelcomeCommandWhenReady`):

1. welcome workspace opens
2. `runWhenInitialTerminalReady` — **initial Ghostty surface spawns a shell** → rc files execute → protected-folder access → prompts fire, attributed to c11
3. `performQuadLayout` (sends `c11 welcome\n`)
4. 1.2 s delay
5. AgentSkills sheet (if relevant)
6. TCC primer sheet

By step 6, the user has already seen five dialogs. The primer explains prompts that already fired.

## Web research (Apr 2026)

- `NS*UsageDescription` keys in Info.plist **do not trigger prompts on their own.** They only customize the prompt copy when the corresponding API is called. Unused keys (Music, Photos, Contacts, Calendar, Reminders, Camera) are cosmetic noise at worst — not the cause of the cascade the user is seeing.
- The cascade is caused by shell activity. When Ghostty spawns a shell, macOS treats c11 as the *responsible process*. Every protected folder the shell's rc files touch fires a prompt. Universal across iTerm2, Warp, Ghostty — documented in [Ghostty Discussion #4496](https://github.com/ghostty-org/ghostty/discussions/4496) and [iTerm2's FDA wiki](https://gitlab.com/gnachman/iterm2/-/wikis/Fulldiskaccess).
- **Full Disk Access is THE industry-accepted fix.** One-time grant, suppresses the entire cascade. The posix_spawn "shed the responsible-process bit" workaround is blocked by `TIOCSCTTY` / `setsid` requirements (Ghostty issue #9263).
- Local Network (macOS 15+) is a separate single prompt, not a cascade. Not in scope here.

## Fix

Two coordinated changes:

### 1. Reorder `sendWelcomeCommandWhenReady` so the primer resolves BEFORE the initial shell spawns

- Welcome workspace is created but the initial terminal pane does not yet spawn a shell.
- TCC primer sheet presents immediately on first launch.
- User chooses Full Disk Access (opens Settings → Privacy & Security) or "Continue without it."
- *Then* the shell spawns, `c11 welcome` prints, quad layout assembles.

Exact call site: `Sources/AppDelegate.swift:6202-6218`. Current order is `runWhenInitialTerminalReady { … performQuadLayout + sendText … presentAgentSkillsOnboarding → chained TCC primer }`. New order: TCC primer first (via `shouldPresent` gate), then Agent Skills (if applicable), then the shell-spawning steps.

### 2. Promote Full Disk Access to primary button in `Sources/TCCPrimerView.swift`

- **Primary**: "Grant Full Disk Access" — opens the Settings pane.
- **Secondary**: "Continue without it" — closes the sheet, shell spawns, cascade may fire.
- Copy refreshed to make FDA the recommended path and acknowledge this is the industry-standard approach for mature terminal emulators.

## Scope

- Fresh installs only. `TCCPrimer.migrateExistingUserIfNeeded` at `AppDelegate.swift:2348` stays as-is — users who already saw the old primer are not re-prompted with the new one.
- English copy changes require xcstrings updates for the six supported locales (ja, uk, ko, zh-Hans, zh-Hant, ru). Delegate to a translator sub-agent after English lands.
- Follow-up ticket tracks FDA runtime detection + auto-continue (the primer currently doesn't notice when the user grants FDA and comes back; user has to click "Continue without it" even though they did grant it).

## Acceptance

- On a fresh install (fresh UserDefaults suite — tagged dev build), launching c11 shows the TCC primer as the first surface, not a shell pane with prompts flying.
- Clicking "Grant Full Disk Access" opens the Settings pane.
- Clicking "Continue without it" closes the sheet and lets the welcome workspace + shell continue.
- Existing users (pre-migration) do NOT see the new primer.
- Localized copy lands for all six locales.
data.title: Reorder first-launch onboarding: TCC primer before initial shell spawn, Full Disk Access as primary CTA

data.body: Orchestration started via lattice-delegate. Orchestrator: surface:73 (tab "C11-15 Orchestrator"). Delegator: pane:45 / surface:77 (tab "C11-15 Delegator"). Delegator prompt at /tmp/C11-15-delegator-prompt.md. Phase sub-agents (Plan, Impl, Translator, Review) will be sibling surfaces in the delegator's pane. Related: C11-16 (FDA runtime-detect + auto-continue, follow-up work out of scope here).
Delegator online. Plan phase starting in sibling surface:78 (pane:45). Monitoring for plan completion before advancing to Impl.

Plan phase complete. Implementation plan written to .lattice/notes/task_01KPYD58ZKBCHHMF4M2GKE6A0E.md. Key decisions: (1) openWelcomeWorkspace is the real change site — shell spawns inside Workspace.init() via addWorkspace, which runs before sendWelcomeCommandWhenReady; deferring addWorkspace into a primer-completion callback is the only reliable gate. (2) New ordering is TCC primer → AgentSkills → workspace creation (reverses current AgentSkills-first chain). (3) 'Grant Full Disk Access' marks shown + opens Settings but does not close the primer window in this ticket — workspace starts when user manually closes it; follow-up ticket adds FDA-grant auto-detection.

Plan complete and reviewed by delegator. Key architectural insight: shell spawns inside Workspace.init() (via addWorkspace in openWelcomeWorkspace), BEFORE sendWelcomeCommandWhenReady is called — so the real fix is deferring addWorkspace to a primer-completion callback in openWelcomeWorkspace, not just reordering within sendWelcomeCommandWhenReady. New ordering: TCC primer → AgentSkills → workspace creation. No human decisions needed. Advancing to impl.

Impl phase starting in sibling surface:79. Plan reviewed and approved by delegator — key insight: shell spawns in Workspace.init() inside openWelcomeWorkspace, so deferring addWorkspace to a primer-completion callback is the correct architectural fix.

Impl complete. Commits: e6e52cef TCCPrimerView: promote FDA to primary CTA, flip button order, refresh copy | 3ae9e00b AppDelegate: add onCompletion callbacks to presentTCCPrimer and presentAgentSkillsOnboarding | b5ad3b30 AppDelegate: defer workspace creation to after primer/AgentSkills in openWelcomeWorkspace | 4f4acf8d Localizable.xcstrings: add English strings for C11-15 button label changes. Build: PASS. Branch: c11-15-tcc-primer-first.
Translator phase started in surface:82. 4 changed English keys (tccPrimer.title, tccPrimer.body.fullDisk, tccPrimer.button.grantFDA, tccPrimer.button.continueWithout) × 6 locales.

Translation complete. 4 keys × 6 locales translated (ja, uk, ko, zh-Hans, zh-Hant, ru). Keys: tccPrimer.title, tccPrimer.body.fullDisk, tccPrimer.button.grantFDA, tccPrimer.button.continueWithout. All set to state: translated. Commit: 1cbc3c82.
Review phase starting in surface:83. 5 commits on c11-15-tcc-primer-first to review against plan + acceptance criteria.

APPROVE. Reviewed 5 commits across TCCPrimerView.swift, AppDelegate.swift, and Localizable.xcstrings.

**Acceptance criteria:** All 8 pass. The architectural change in openWelcomeWorkspace correctly defers addWorkspace (and therefore Workspace.init() / shell spawn) into a completion callback, so no PTY exists until after primer dismissal. sendWelcomeCommandWhenReady no longer has the 1.2s asyncAfter chain. 'Grant Full Disk Access' is .primary (right), correctly sets shownKey + opens FDA pane without closing the window; 'Continue without it' is .secondary (left), sets shownKey + closes. migrateExistingUserIfNeeded untouched.

**CLAUDE.md constraints:** All pass. No --no-verify, no hot-path touches (hitTest, forceRefresh, TabItemView unchanged), Info.plist untouched, all Text()/Button() calls use String(localized:defaultValue:), no bare literals. Only the 3 expected files changed.

**Localization:** All 4 changed/new keys (tccPrimer.button.grantFDA, tccPrimer.button.continueWithout, tccPrimer.title, tccPrimer.body.fullDisk) have state:translated across all 6 locales (ja/uk/ko/zh-Hans/zh-Hant/ru).

**Code quality:** continueWelcomeWorkspaceSetup has correct window-nil guard. onCompletion fires from willClose in both functions, covering both button and red-close-button paths. Capture semantics correct throughout — [weak self] in outer closures, [weak window] where needed, no cycles. 0.35s delay preserved in AgentSkills completion.

Minor (non-blocking): old keys tccPrimer.button.gotIt and tccPrimer.button.openSettings are orphaned in xcstrings — cleanup in follow-up.

Implemented and reviewed clean. Root fix was architectural: shell spawns inside Workspace.init() (via addWorkspace in openWelcomeWorkspace), so the only reliable gate is deferring addWorkspace to a primer-completion callback — not reordering within sendWelcomeCommandWhenReady. Changes: (1) openWelcomeWorkspace now presents TCC primer → AgentSkills → then creates workspace via continueWelcomeWorkspaceSetup; (2) sendWelcomeCommandWhenReady simplified — 1.2s asyncAfter onboarding chain removed; (3) TCCPrimerView flips buttons (FDA primary, Continue Without secondary) and refreshes copy to frame FDA as the recommended path; (4) 4 xcstrings keys translated to 6 locales. Build: PASS. Hot paths untouched. Fresh-install gate (migrateExistingUserIfNeeded) untouched. One follow-up noted: orphaned tccPrimer.button.gotIt and tccPrimer.button.openSettings keys to clean up.

C11-15 complete. Shipped: architectural fix deferring addWorkspace to primer-completion callback in openWelcomeWorkspace (shell no longer spawns before primer); TCCPrimerView FDA as primary CTA with refreshed copy; 4 xcstrings keys translated × 6 locales. Build: PASS. Review: APPROVE. PR #70: https://github.com/Stage-11-Agentics/c11/pull/70 (base: c11-13-tab-bar-chrome-states). Follow-up: C11-16 for FDA runtime auto-detect.
